description:
The bird was a bit smaller and more slender than
a Robin.  Both male and female Bullock’s
Orioles were present at the same time, size and shape were the same.  The bird had a dark pointed bill, dark eyes,
and black cowl over the entire head. 
Orange feathers extended from the breast to the belly and
undertail.  Legs were dark.  The upper tail feathers were orange toward
the tips and black toward the rump.  When
the bird flew one could see that the central retices were black all the way to
the tips and the rump was orange.  Wings
were black overall with orange extending into the median coverts.  Black greater coverts were tipped in white
and some white streaking ran lengthwise through the flight feathers.
photographer: Photographer: Robin Henry, Robin Henry, Robin Henry, Robin Henry, Robin Henry
